candidates.title: Update trust center and public docs to reflect v0.6.4 as current public release
candidates.source: docs/trust/README.md, docs/security/release-readiness.md, docs/public-launch-readiness.md, and docs/trust/v0.6.4-status.md still describe v0.6.4 as not yet tagged or v0.6.3 as current public release
candidates.acceptance_criteria: All public-release references resolve to v0.6.4 as current; stale not-yet-tagged wording is removed.
candidates.reason: Low-cost docs consistency that prevents reviewer confusion.

candidates.title: Normalize/fix stale PUBLIC_TAG and package/tag version references in checkers
candidates.source: scripts/check_version_consistency.py, scripts/check_trust_center.py, scripts/check_public_launch_readiness.py, and related tests still hardcode v0.6.3 as the current public tag even though v0.6.4 is released.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: All checker constants reference v0.6.4 as current public tag and 0.6.4 as package version; stale v0.6.3 current-release claims are rejected by checkers.
candidates.reason: Prevents recurring post-release test failures and checker drift.

candidates.title: Regression test for security_md_current package-version validation
candidates.source: f3972ef fixed release_assurance.py but has no dedicated regression test for the package-version vs tag-version distinction.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Test verifies that security_md_current passes when SECURITY.md contains the package version and fails when it contains only a mismatched tag version.

candidates.title: Update release checklist version references
candidates.source: docs/release-checklist.md contains example commands with hardcoded historical versions that drift after each release.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Examples reference the current source version and public tag without stale historical values.

candidates.title: Harden deterministic ordering in JSON outputs
candidates.source: Some evidence or report generators may still use unsorted collections when producing JSON, causing flaky cross-platform diffs.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: All JSON-producing report modules use deterministic ordering (sort_keys=True or explicit sorting).
candidates.reason: Most JSON outputs already use sort_keys=True; remaining cases are in complex research modules where a dedicated determinism pass is more appropriate than a patch-scope change.

candidates.title: v0.6.5 release prep checker skeleton
candidates.source: Following the pattern of check_v064_release_prep.py, prepare a skeleton that validates absence of v0.6.5 artifacts while source version remains 0.6.4.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Planning mode rejects docs/releases/v0.6.5.md and 0.6.5 version bump; release-prep mode will accept them after bump.
candidates.reason: Prepares the release-prep gate without enabling it prematurely.

rejected.title: Live trading default changes
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: Broker execution enablement
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: Provider execution unlock
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: PyPI publish
rejected.reason: Not approved in this planning batch; requires separate process.

rejected.title: Automatic skill activation
rejected.reason: Skill candidates remain manually reviewed and offline.

rejected.title: Automatic learning execution
rejected.reason: Learning suggestions remain offline and manually reviewed.

safety_boundaries: Live trading remains disabled by default.
Live submit remains disabled by default.
Provider execution remains disabled by default.
Broker execution remains disabled by default.
Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.
Human approval remains required for live order flow.
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured.
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
constraints: Version is not bumped to 0.6.5 in this batch
No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
release_criteria: dev_check, ci_check, and release_check --quick pass locally
CI on main is green after the prep commit is pushed
Version is bumped to 0.6.5 in pyproject.toml and src/atlas_agent/__init__.py
scripts/check_v065_release_prep.py and matching tests are created
Owner approval is obtained for tag cutover
